sources.notes: Interactive statement for recovering the hidden width in the easy version.
Official editorial confirms the two-query interval narrowing and candidate encoding idea.

annotation.summary: Recover a hidden parameter through a very small number of interactive queries.
annotation.constraints: Only a few queries are allowed; the easy limits permit a direct encoded second query after narrowing the candidate interval.
annotation.core_idea: First ask a uniform query that turns the answer into a coarse interval for the hidden width. Then choose a second query whose values encode the remaining candidates so the response identifies the exact one.
annotation.complexity: O(1) queries plus O(K) candidate preparation for the small interval
tricks: Use a coarse query to shrink the candidate range.
Encode candidates in the second query so answers are distinguishable.

solution_variants.name: two-query-candidate-encoding
solution_variants.summary: Use one uniform probe to restrict the answer and one crafted probe to distinguish all remaining possibilities.

tags.evidence: The core task is reconstructing a hidden value from the interactive judge's oracle responses.
The second query is deliberately designed so different hidden candidates produce distinguishable answers.
tags.description: Design query values so that oracle responses encode enough information to distinguish a finite candidate set.
tags.created_reason: This interactive encoding pattern is reusable and more specific than a generic interactive reconstruction tag.

tags.evidence: The response interpretation depends on quotient/divisibility behavior of the hidden width.
The solution constructs a query sequence with prescribed distinguishing behavior.
